expectations.evidence: Files are split into distinct logical concerns: 01-prisma-schema-update.md (data layer), 02-api-profile-photo-upload.md (avatar API), 03-api-update-nickname.md (nickname API), 04-api-change-password.md (password API), and 05-frontend-profile-page.md (UI). Each file addresses a single coherent domain rather than mixing concerns arbitrarily.

Numbered prefixes 01-05 reflect execution dependencies: '01-prisma-schema-update' is first because 'This schema change is the foundation that all other profile-related API routes and UI components depend on.' API routes (02-04) follow the schema change, and the frontend (05) comes last as it depends on the APIs.

Every file has a context paragraph. Examples: file 01: 'The user profile feature requires extending the existing User model in Prisma to support a profile photo URL and nickname field. This schema change is the foundation that all other profile-related API routes and UI components depend on.' File 02: 'Create a Next.js API route that handles profile photo uploads. The uploaded file should be stored...and the resulting URL should be persisted to the avatarUrl field on the User record via Prisma.'

Items specify exact file paths, commands, and validation rules. Examples: 'Run npx prisma migrate dev --name add-user-profile-fields to generate and apply the migration', 'Validate file type (allow only image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp) and enforce a max file size of 5 MB', 'Use bcrypt.compare(currentPassword, user.passwordHash) to verify the current password; return 401 if it does not match', 'Hash the new password using bcrypt.hash(newPassword, 12)'

File 01-prisma-schema-update.md is entirely dedicated to schema/model changes: adding 'nickname String?' and 'avatarUrl String?' fields, running migrations, and regenerating the Prisma client. The API route implementations in files 02-04 are fully separate and only reference the fields via prisma.user.update calls.

None of the 5 output files mention test file paths or describe specific test scenarios. There are no references to test files (e.g., no '*.test.ts' or '*.spec.ts' paths), no testing frameworks, and no test case descriptions anywhere in the checklist items.

File 02 hedges with 'The uploaded file should be stored (e.g., on the local filesystem under public/uploads or a cloud storage service)' rather than stating a firm architectural assumption. The use of 'e.g.' and 'or a cloud storage service' means no clear assumption is committed to. No other file states explicit architectural assumptions such as 'file uploads stored locally, no external storage assumed.'

File 05 names three components (AvatarUpload.tsx, NicknameForm.tsx, PasswordForm.tsx) and describes their behavior, but does not specify any TypeScript prop interfaces. There is no definition like 'AvatarUpload accepts currentAvatarUrl: string | null, onSuccess: (newUrl: string) => void' or equivalent prop signatures for any of the components.
